reviews.review_description:
Audience booing every time you miss made me uninstall the game. 
Don't care if other modes are better this is just discouraging and disgusting.
How about make the crowd sound like they are shocked or caring and supporting when you fail?
Great experience. Probably Will buy complete game.

reviews.review_title: Really Fun, but some major flaws
reviews.review_description:
This is one of the funnest games that I have played thus far on Quest.  However it currently has some major flaws.

Firstly it crashes roughly 1 out of 3 times that I try to start it.  Usually requiring me to completely restart my Quest.  Which is frustrating considering the second flaw.  Which is that this program takes forever to startup.   Then once it has started, you still have to go through multiple menus before you can even start playing the game.  Some of these menus require you to pause and listen to a tip, or listen to the announcer say the same thing he says every time.  There is no way to skip any of this.

One of the most powerful features of the Oculus Quest is how quickly you can start playing games as compared to the Rift.  This is because you can just grab it, put it on, and start playing.  Sadly, this game undoes all of those advantages.

If you are going to play you had better plan on three or four minutes before you can even start this game, and that is if it doesn't crash.  If it crashes, then you will have an additional four or five minutes to restart the quest and then open the app again.

Having said that, this is a really fun and addictive game.  One of the best on the platform.  Hoping that they fix all the issues so that it can be truly great.

"Yeah, handsome, great big guy, seven feet tall! Name is Rick Miller — Portland, Oregon. And he started a business. Of course you know it was in basketball. But it wasn't in basketball! I mean, I figured he had to be in sport, but he wasn't in sport!"

First game that got me out of the barrier and got myself hurt.
Why design a full tennis court and send balls just outside the safe area?

I played games like Thrill of the Fight jumping and running around and never had issues
this game was so fun that i wanna buy the full game now

reviews.review_title: i will buy the full game only if -
reviews.review_description: can i have a mode without the scramble? i don't want the tennis racket to turn into golf stick... already Oculus Quest can't track the controller moving beyond 200 degrees
this is a very nice game that i woud like to have on the rift  so i can play with other friends thanks alot and keep op the good work

Just got the quest system and am trying out the demos.  This demo allowed one bowling demo, and a few tennis, but I cant try the baseball demo.  Everytime I go back to restart, it takes me directly to tennis mode.  Looks fun, but I'm not convinced enough to buy it.  Maybe later if support tells me whats wrong.

reviews.review_title: Should be fun, too many bugs
reviews.review_description:
This game has potential, but has way to many bugs.  After a couple dozen games, here's what I've found
Baseball:
- Your fielders make errors on fly balls 40% of the time, cpu hardly ever.
- Throw a pitch in the dirt that the CPU swings on, it's still called a ball.
- CPU hits home runs almost every pitch when the get the golden bat. I've had them home run on a pitch at their feet and 2 feet out of the strike zone. Once they get the golden bat expect to lose by 15-20 runs
- When hitting left, I always have to use the back of the gold club, cricket bat, etc.
- When hitting (left) it makes me move to the edge of guardian instead of allowing me to be in the middle or (even better) right side. It means every swing triggers the guadian grid to appear and if I have to reach for a pitch I'm going outside the strike zone.
Tennis
- Often hits the ball outside of the safe zone, making a force miss or a dangerous play.
General:
- Settings are not saved, they need to be reset every time.

I really think this game has a lot of potential and find it fun even with these bugs, but the bugs make it too frustrating to play.

UPDATE: Still loving the games, play them almost daily, but found one flaw. If you go into passthrough mode, or sometimes just break through your boundary wall, one or both of your controllers may not be recognized by the game - sometimes invisible, other times just hanging in air. You have use the Oculus button to get out of the game, then come back in. Doesn't happen often enough to drop a star off my review.

I totally cannot play any of these sports IRL (you know, the serious versions), nor even that well in the game, but these are so wacky. I have a ton of fun losing, and even more fun when I do win. I don't have the room to give the tennis game a fair evaluation, but the bowling and baseball are a blast. Like another reviewer, I don't need live competition (they'd either be super frustrated by my ineptitude, or die laughing), so the bots are fine for me.

I love this game. Awesome moments practicing sports, exercising and having fun!
 
Downloaded the demo and liked. Bought the game and it offers much more! The scramble mode is fun, but you can turn it off to play sports more realistically. 

Even the complains that some users made (like long intro and bad flow) were already corrected by the developers. 

I really recommend to buy this game! Download the demo and try it now.

this game i think is very good. Even that good i dont need the normal version. I dont want to play online bots for me are fine! I love the bowling tennis is a bit meh because of unfair golf clubs and baseball i cant play. Overall for free this is a good option for people who like to stick to the bots!

reviews.review_title: You need a football field size barrier..
reviews.review_description:
This started out fun, but I learned very fast that you need a large barrier for this game to work.  The ball went very wide and before I could get it I not only stepped out of my barrier, I tripped over my couch and hurt myself because it didn't have time to shut the game down and warn me.  Demo promptly deleted and here's a warning to anyone looking at this:  Have a very large playable area.  

The game should warn you if your barrier isn't big enough like Vader does.  I have no problems in any other game I've tried yet but this one is a bust if you are in a somewhat confined space.  Ouch.
